 {"id":"bd-3zm7","title":"bd mol advance: Step through molecule execution","description":"Implement bd mol advance for stepping through molecules.

## Command

```bash
bd mol advance <mol-id>
```

## Behavior

1. Load molecule state from .beads/molecules/<mol-id>.state.yaml
2. Find current step
3. Mark current step completed
4. Find next available step (respecting needs/deps)
5. Update state file
6. Output next step info (or COMPLETE if done)

## Step State Machine

```
pending → in_progress → completed
```

When advancing:
- Current step: in_progress → completed
- Next step: pending → in_progress

## Output

```bash
$ bd mol advance mol-deacon-patrol
✓ Step inbox-check completed
→ Next step: spawn-work

Title: Spawn polecat for ready work
Description: ...
```

Or if complete:
```bash
$ bd mol advance mol-deacon-patrol
✓ Step self-inspect completed
✓ Molecule COMPLETE

Run bd mol reset to loop, or gt handoff to cycle.
```

## Files

- cmd/bd/mol_advance.go

 {"id":"bd-40c2","title":"bd init --from-jsonl: Skip git history scan","description":"## Problem

`bd init` scans git history to find all issues ever created, which:
- Resurrects deleted/compacted issues
- Takes a long time on large repos
- Defeats manual JSONL cleanup

## Solution

Add `bd init --from-jsonl` flag that:
1. Creates fresh database
2. Imports ONLY from current .beads/issues.jsonl
3. Skips git history scan entirely

## Use Case

After manually cleaning JSONL or receiving a clean JSONL from another source:
```bash
# Clean JSONL manually or via script
bd init --from-jsonl  # Use current JSONL as-is
```

(Moved from hq-c21fj)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-02T01:37:20.481211-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe","updated_at":"2026-01-02T12:41:30.784964-08:00","closed_at":"2026-01-02T12:41:30.784964-08:00","close_reason":"Duplicate of bd-c0b6"}
